title: About Us
subtitle: Serving Champasari, Siliguri since 1983

seo.title: About Jatiya Shakti Sangha
seo.description: The story of Jatiya Shakti Sangha O Pathagar — a community and cultural club founded in 1983 in Champasari, Siliguri.

body:
One fine evening in the year 1983, a group of young people from this locality — led by Sri Swapan Chakraborty, Sri Dibyendu Sanyal and others — shared an idea while a bridegroom's party had come to marry one of our elder sisters. They proposed some financial help for forming a club in the locality. The bridegroom's party responded with great interest and donated **Rs. 500** for the purpose.

That same year, one of our well-known youths was suddenly lost to electrocution. In his memory, and with the donation given by the bridegroom's party, our club came into existence as **Jatiya Shakti Sangha O Pathagar**. A small plot of about 4 kathas along the Champasari Main Road was arranged with the positive support of Roy Jethamashai, the National Award-winning teacher Late Shailendra Kumar Sen, and Sri Arabinda Biswas, the then President of the Siliguri–Naxalbari Panchayat Samity.

With the help of its members, a small club house was built, and over the years it has grown into a double-storied building. The club is not confined to social service alone — it strives to be an outstanding force for preserving the cultural heritage and tranquility of the area, and a leading organisation for a green environment through culture, sports and education.

With the constant and sustained efforts of its members — and the keen support of every section of society — the club opened a **Table Tennis Training Centre**, inaugurated by Sri Ashok Bhattacharjee, Hon'ble Minister for Urban Development & Municipal Affairs, Government of West Bengal, along with the noted Bengal cricketer Md. Saba Karim.

title: Club Profile
subtitle: At a glance

seo.title: Club Profile
seo.description: Profile of Jatiya Shakti Sangha O Pathagar, Champasari, Siliguri.

body:
**Jatiya Shakti Sangha O Pathagar** is a community, cultural and social welfare club based in Champasari, Siliguri, in the Darjeeling district of West Bengal.

- **Established:** 1983
- **Silver Jubilee:** 2007
- **Location:** Champasari Main Road, Siliguri, Darjeeling
- **Focus areas:** Durga Puja & cultural festivals, sports, social service, a public library (pathagar), health camps and community welfare.

_This section is being expanded. Please keep visiting for more details._

title: Sports
subtitle: Encouraging the young talent of our area

seo.title: Sports
seo.description: Cricket, table tennis and dance programmes run by Jatiya Shakti Sangha.

body:
Alongside our social activities, we pay special attention to sports to encourage the children of this area.

- A running **cricket tournament** is held every year, with most of the prominent clubs of Siliguri, Malda, Coochbehar and Alipurduar taking part.
- A **table tennis tournament** is hosted under the banner of the Siliguri District Table Tennis Association and the Siliguri Municipal Corporation.
- **Table tennis coaching** is imparted to about 100 children of the area under the guidance of an eminent player of the district.
- **Bharatanatyam** is taught on a weekly basis by a renowned Bharatanatyam dancer of Siliguri.

title: Social Activities
subtitle: Working for a greener, healthier community

seo.title: Social Activities
seo.description: Plantation drives, awareness campaigns and community service by Jatiya Shakti Sangha.

body:
### Making our surroundings green

With the rising effects of global warming, the time has come to reduce its impact to the greatest extent possible. Every year we organise regular campaigns for large-scale plantation, planting different varieties of trees across the area, and holding seminars among locals to spread awareness of the harm of felling trees and the benefits of planting them.

Members of the club plant trees across all the school premises of the area, and the school authorities are entrusted to look after their growth and maintenance through mutual understanding.

Wherever unauthorised felling of trees is noticed, club members help prevent it with the support of the Forest Department. A new plan for planting **medicinal trees** is being considered, and Ayurvedic medicine manufacturers — especially in Uttarakhand — are being contacted for this purpose. The club premises themselves are planted with many varieties of valuable fruit trees.

The club also takes a positive role by participating in every social campaign organised by the Siliguri Municipal Corporation, the Forest Department and other agencies.

title: Health Check-ups
subtitle: Free camps for those who need them most

seo.title: Health Check-ups
seo.description: Free eye-testing, blood-donation camps and patient support by Jatiya Shakti Sangha.

body:
- An important step has been taken by organising frequent free health check-ups — including **free eye testing** with the help of prominent doctors of Siliguri — for the poor people of the area and senior citizens who cannot afford costly treatment.
- **Blood-donation camps** are organised on every Red Letter Day, where most members of the club donate blood for the needy.
- **Poor patients are supported financially** for treatment outside the region, including arrangement of rail/air tickets by the club and comprehensive information about hospitals and treatment centres.

title: Library (Pathagar)
subtitle: Books and learning for every student

seo.title: Library
seo.description: The library (pathagar) of Jatiya Shakti Sangha, planned to serve poor and meritorious students.

body:
A free library for poor students is central to our mission. Our activities cover a large, densely populated area that includes many poor yet meritorious students who cannot fully pursue their education for want of facilities.

A library is being developed with a target of at least **5,000 books** of various standards, including reference books, to benefit the maximum number of poor students of this area — free of cost.

_This section is being expanded. Please keep visiting for more details._

title: Future Plans
subtitle: What we are building next

seo.title: Future Plans
seo.description: Upcoming plans of Jatiya Shakti Sangha — library, health camps and a community hall.

body:
Our club is already associated with numerous social programmes organised by various Government and non-Government agencies. Beyond these, some added permanent facilities are planned in the coming years.

### a) A library for poor students — free of cost
With many poor, meritorious students in our area, we are designing a library of at least 5,000 books of various standards, including reference books, to benefit the maximum number of students.

### b) Free health check-up camps with visiting doctors
We already run frequent free health check-up camps with reputed doctors of Siliguri. Efforts are on to bring in doctors who visit Siliguri on an occasional basis.

### c) A community hall
With the completion of the club house, a portion will be opened as a **community hall** available for a minimum cleanliness charge — helping poorer families organise their domestic functions at low cost.

seo.title: Members & Membership
seo.description: Become a member of Jatiya Shakti Sangha. Download the membership form.

body:
Our members are the backbone of the club. We welcome new members who wish to contribute to the cultural, sporting and social life of Champasari.

To apply, please download and fill in the membership form below and submit it at the club house.
